description:
Goal: switch compiler2 front doors onto the in-house native backend and delete the compiler2 route through old-world codegen entry points. Signal: `Compiler2::compile_root_jit`, `run_root_jit`, `compile_root_aot`, compiler2 tests, and the `fz2` binary use only the compiler2-owned backend path; `src/compiler2/compiler.rs` no longer creates a fresh legacy type owner or calls the planner-shaped shared native entrypoint.

Change:
- move compiler2 JIT/AOT/runtime entry points onto the compiler2-owned backend
- delete compiler2-specific dead helpers and entry paths in the old shared native route
- keep shared only the universal backend/runtime substrate still consumed by both worlds

Why:
- until the front doors switch, the compiler2 path still pays the old-world tax even if the new backend exists

Checks:
- compiler2 front-door and CLI tests pass through the in-house backend
- grep for compiler2 callsites into the old shared native entrypoint is empty
title: Compiler2 native backends: switch compiler2 front doors and delete the old route
